text: On 12 March 2024, France and Kenya signed a bilateral health-sector aid agreement worth EUR 80 million over four years, focused on maternal and child health programs in five Kenyan counties.
The European Union and Vietnam concluded a free trade agreement on 30 June 2019, eliminating 99 percent of tariffs on goods over a ten-year transition period.
Germany and Japan announced on 15 January 2023 a joint defense cooperation pact covering intelligence sharing, joint training exercises, and supply-chain resilience for semiconductors.
Brazil and Argentina signed a bilateral climate accord in November 2022 committing both countries to a coordinated reduction in Amazon deforestation by 2030.
The United States and South Korea finalized a renewed Status of Forces Agreement in October 2021, updating cost-sharing arrangements for US military presence on the Korean Peninsula.
Norway and Indonesia extended their REDD+ partnership through 2025 with an additional USD 1 billion in performance-based payments tied to verified emissions reductions.
India and Australia entered into the Economic Cooperation and Trade Agreement on 2 April 2022, providing tariff-free access for 96 percent of Australian exports to India.
On 8 September 2020, the United Arab Emirates and Israel signed the Abraham Accords, normalizing diplomatic relations and establishing cooperation in security, technology, and tourism.
Canada and Chile renewed their bilateral mining cooperation framework in March 2023, focused on critical minerals supply chains and joint research on lithium extraction.
The World Health Organization and Rwanda signed a health-systems strengthening agreement on 14 May 2024, providing technical assistance for the rollout of a national digital health platform.
In April 2018, Mexico, Canada, and the United States agreed to the USMCA, replacing NAFTA and updating provisions on automotive content, dairy, and digital trade.
South Africa and Egypt signed a memorandum of understanding on agricultural trade on 22 February 2023, covering grain imports and joint extension services.
The United Kingdom and Singapore concluded a digital economy agreement on 25 February 2022, covering cross-border data flows, electronic invoicing, and AI governance principles.
On 3 December 2021, Pakistan and China renewed the China-Pakistan Economic Corridor memorandum, allocating an additional USD 11 billion to energy and transport infrastructure projects.
The Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank approved a USD 600 million loan to the Philippines in July 2023 for the Metro Manila Subway, in coordination with the Japan International Cooperation Agency.
France and Senegal signed a security cooperation agreement on 9 November 2022, focused on counterterrorism training and equipment provision in the Sahel.
In June 2024, the G7 finance ministers reached an agreement to leverage frozen Russian sovereign assets to provide a USD 50 billion loan to Ukraine.
Vietnam and Russia formalized a strategic partnership accord on 20 June 2024, including continued defense procurement and oil and gas joint ventures in the South China Sea.
The African Continental Free Trade Area agreement entered into force on 1 January 2021, with 54 of 55 African Union member states as signatories at launch.
On 11 November 2019, the United States and China signed a phase-one trade deal halting further tariff escalations and committing China to USD 200 billion in additional US imports.
Switzerland and the European Union reached a framework agreement in March 2024 governing institutional relations, free movement of persons, and dispute resolution mechanisms.
Saudi Arabia and Iran restored diplomatic relations on 10 March 2023 in a deal brokered by China, ending a seven-year diplomatic rupture.
Bangladesh and Bhutan signed a preferential trade agreement in December 2020 covering 100 Bhutanese exports and 34 Bangladeshi exports under reduced tariff schedules.
The Inter-American Development Bank announced a USD 1.4 billion financing package for Colombia in February 2023 covering rural connectivity and renewable energy projects.
In October 2022, Australia and Japan signed a Reciprocal Access Agreement allowing the deployment of defense forces to each other's territory for joint training exercises.
On 7 July 2023, the European Union and New Zealand signed a free trade agreement eliminating 91 percent of tariffs and including a binding chapter on the Paris climate goals.
Ethiopia, Kenya, and Somalia held a tripartite summit in April 2024 and announced a regional security framework, but the joint declaration did not specify financing or troop commitments.
Saudi Arabia announced an investment partnership with Pakistan on 27 March 2024, the size of which was reported as up to USD 25 billion across mining, energy, and IT, with details to be finalized.
The Caribbean Community and the Dominican Republic concluded an agreement in May 2023 on regulatory harmonization for goods trade, services, and investment.
In November 2023, COP28 closed in Dubai with a consensus declaration calling for transitioning away from fossil fuels in energy systems, signed by 198 parties.
On 18 January 2025, Italy and Tunisia signed a migration management agreement worth EUR 100 million in budget support tied to border control cooperation.
The United States and Vietnam upgraded relations to a comprehensive strategic partnership in September 2023, covering semiconductors, climate, and supply chains.
Cambodia and Thailand renewed a bilateral border trade pact in June 2024, covering customs facilitation and a list of 80 designated border markets.
On 15 May 2022, Israel and India announced a memorandum of understanding on cyber-defense cooperation, including a joint working group and information-sharing protocols.
The Pacific Islands Forum endorsed a 2050 Strategy for the Blue Pacific Continent in July 2022, identifying climate adaptation, ocean health, and people-centered development as priorities.
Following months of talks, Egypt and Ethiopia did not reach a final agreement on the Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam by the end of 2023, though both governments confirmed continued negotiations.
The European Investment Bank and Tunisia signed a EUR 300 million loan in April 2023 for the upgrade of the country's electricity transmission grid.
On 14 February 2024, the United States, Japan, and Australia announced a trilateral cybersecurity initiative targeting critical infrastructure protection in the Indo-Pacific.
The Republic of Korea and Saudi Arabia signed a strategic partnership accord on 22 October 2023, including USD 30 billion in announced project commitments across energy and construction.
Colombia and Venezuela reopened a bilateral commerce framework on 26 September 2022, restoring diplomatic relations after three years and resuming cross-border trade routes.
On 4 April 2023, Finland formally joined NATO as the 31st member state, completing the accession process initiated after the Russian invasion of Ukraine.
The Trans-Pacific Partnership members admitted the United Kingdom as a new member in July 2023, marking the first accession since the original signatories.
Mongolia and Korea signed a critical minerals supply agreement in September 2023, covering rare earth elements and joint research on extraction technologies.
On 9 September 2023, the African Union became a permanent member of the G20 at the New Delhi summit, on a par with the European Union.
Spain and Morocco normalized diplomatic relations and signed a bilateral cooperation roadmap on 7 April 2022, covering migration, trade, and Western Sahara consultations.
In late 2023, the United Kingdom announced an AI Safety Institute partnership with the United States and ten other countries, though formal treaty status remains unspecified.
The Eurasian Economic Union and Iran signed a free trade agreement on 25 December 2023, covering most goods at preferential tariffs over a three-year transition.
On 11 May 2022, Sri Lanka and India formalized a USD 4 billion line of credit covering fuel imports, food imports, and emergency budget support.
Nigeria, Niger, and Algeria signed a memorandum of understanding in July 2022 to advance the Trans-Saharan Gas Pipeline, with feasibility studies funded jointly by the three governments.
On 14 December 2023, the European Union member states adopted the AI Act, the first comprehensive horizontal regulation of artificial intelligence systems, with phased entry into force through 2026.
